title: IPv4 Address Structure and Classes
description: A visual breakdown of the 32-bit IPv4 address format, showing its division into octets, the network and host portions, and the five primary address classes (A, B, C, D, E).
relationships: The 32 bits are grouped into 4 octets
The first octet's bits determine the address class
The class defines the split between the network and host portions of the address
Classes A, B, and C are used for general unicast addressing
